mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any 2-year rolling window, the average 20-day forward return following PAYEMS increases is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after trigger events, the average 20-day forward return is non-negative or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based demand strength, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). The signal requires both series to rise jointly.
failure_condition: If instances where both HOUST and RSAFS increase over the prior month do not produce significantly positive 60-day returns (t-test p>0.05) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) is a positive signal for equities only when monetary policy is accommodative (FEDFUNDS below 4.0%). In such a regime, rising output supports earnings growth; in tight regimes, supply growth may coincide with demand weakness, making the signal unreliable.
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS < 4.0, INDPRO increases do not lead to positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of cases over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly retail sales (RSAFS) increases reflect robust consumer demand, a primary driver of U.S. economic growth, and are positively associated with S&P 500 returns over the next week (5 trading days).
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following RSAFS increases is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE rising more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months) signals labor market deterioration that erodes consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: If after such trigger events, the 60-day forward return is not negative on average (t-test p>0.05) over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When both the price level (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) are above their respective 12-month moving averages, it indicates rising inflation met with monetary tightening, a stagflationary mix that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in triggered months, the 20-day forward return is not negative with at least 55% frequency over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) of more than 2% over a 3-month period signals a contraction in manufacturing and aggregate supply, foreshadowing economic weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after such decline events, the 20-day forward return is not negative on average (t-test p>0.05) over any 5-year period.
